_description: Wide-format (16:9) data-highlight card for presentations, YouTube thumbnails, and desktop content. Two-column layout places the oversized metric on the left with context and detail on the right. Supports optional background images with automatic dark scrim. Each slide should vary 'bg' and 'accent' for visual rhythm.

_slots.eyebrow: optional — short uppercase tag in the top-left. 1–4 words. E.g. 'Key Finding', 'Q3 2024'. Displayed in monospace at the top bar.
_slots.metric: REQUIRED — the hero number or statistic. 1–5 characters for maximum impact at 180px. E.g. '73%', '$4.2T', '10×', '1 in 3'.
_slots.context: REQUIRED — one sentence explaining the metric. 4–12 words. Displayed in the right column at 42px.
_slots.detail: optional — elaboration paragraph. 1–2 sentences adding nuance or comparison. Shown at reduced opacity below a dot separator in the right column.
_slots.source: optional — data source attribution in the bottom-left bar. Shown uppercase in monospace. E.g. 'McKinsey, 2024'.
_slots.bg-image: optional — background image. Local path, URL, or data URI. A diagonal dark gradient scrim is applied automatically. Works best with atmospheric, non-busy photos.
_slots.bg: optional — hex background color. Default #0b0a09. Used when no bg-image is set. Dark near-blacks work best.
_slots.accent: optional — hex accent color for the metric, vertical rule, eyebrow, corners, and dots. Default #ff6b35 (vivid orange).
_slots.ink: optional — hex text color for context, detail, source, and counter. Default #f0ece4 (warm white).

title: The State of Work 2024

slides.context: of employees would quit for better workplace culture
slides.detail: Culture now outranks compensation as the #1 driver of attrition across every industry surveyed.

slides.context: projected global AI market size by 2030
slides.detail: That's a 13× increase from 2023 levels — faster than the mobile revolution.

slides.context: the warming threshold we are about to cross
slides.detail: Every fraction of a degree matters — 1.5° vs 2° means 10 million fewer people exposed to severe heat.

slides.context: cheaper to retain than to replace a senior engineer
startups fail because they build something nobody wants
slides.detail: The most common cause of failure isn't money — it's building without listening.
